channel.title: NYT > Top Stories
channel.copyright: Copyright 2026 The New York Times Company

item.title: How the Fight Over Israel Is Playing Out Inside MAGA
item.description: The war in Iran has added to a tectonic shift in public opinion — a bipartisan swing away from Israel. Some on the far-right are fighting to keep President Trump’s movement aligned with the Jewish state.
item.dc:creator: Anton Troianovski
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:credit: Annie Flanagan for The New York Times
item.media:description: Laura Loomer, the far-right media figure, has emerged as one of the president’s most aggressive, pro-Israel enforcers.

item.title: Attack on French Cargo Ship Highlights Continued Risks in Strait of Hormuz
item.description: U.S. Central Command and the ship’s owner disagreed on whether the vessel coordinated safe passage with the United States military.
item.dc:creator: Peter Eavis
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:description: Vessels in the Strait of Hormuz off the coast of Oman on Wednesday.

item.title: Iran’s Oil Sector and Economy Are Under Pressure as U.S. Blockade Bites
item.description: Tehran says it can endure the pain, but its oil may soon have nowhere to go.
item.dc:creator: Farnaz Fassihi
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:credit: Fatemeh Bahrami/Anadolu Agency, via Getty Images
item.media:description: With the United States blockading Iranian shipping, Iran risks running out of room to store oil in places like Kharg Island.

item.title: Ted Turner, Creator of CNN and the 24-Hour News Cycle, Dies at 87
item.description: As one of the most important figures in media history, he oversaw a vast cable empire of news, sports and entertainment channels.
item.dc:creator: Jonathan Kandell
item.media:description: Ted Turner was a visionary media mogul who dominated the cable television industry, and extended his reach into movies, major league baseball and even yachting.

item.title: Rupert Murdoch, David Zaslav and More Media Moguls Remember Ted Turner, CNN’s Founder
item.description: The CNN founder’s death was met with encomiums from leaders in the media industry, which he helped reinvent. “He will be remembered for generations,” said one.
item.dc:creator: Benjamin Mullin
item.media:credit: Brian F Alpert/ZUMA Press Wire, via Shutterstock
item.media:description: Ted Turner in the 1980s.

item.title: Higher Gas Prices Are Hitting Lower-Income Americans the Hardest
item.description: People with lower incomes are driving less and devoting more of their budgets to transport as gas prices soar.
item.dc:creator: Talmon Joseph Smith
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
Federal Reserve Bank of New York
item.media:description: Gas prices have soared since the start of the war with Iran.

item.title: Why Is Gas So Much More Expensive in Some States Than Others?
item.description: Shipping, taxes, competition and environmental regulations all play a part in the cost of gasoline. That can lead to stark differences in pump prices.
item.dc:creator: Emmett Lindner
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:description: The national average gas price has soared about 50 percent since the start of the war with Iran.

item.title: Tennessee Republicans Unveil New Congressional Map Carving Up Majority-Black House District
item.description: The General Assembly is expected to quickly approve the map, which slices up Memphis, a majority-Black city that makes up most of the state’s lone Democratic district.
item.dc:creator: Emily Cochrane
item.media:credit: Brad J. Vest for The New York Times
item.media:description: From left, Stacey Abrams, a former Georgia state representative, U.S. Representative Steve Cohen, Tennessee State Senator Raumesh Akbari and Tennessee State Senator London Lamar meet after Republican legislators in Tennessee unveiled a new House map.

item.title: Obama Talks Trump, Mamdani, Aliens and More in Colbert Interview
item.description: The former president was critical of President Trump, without ever naming him. Mr. Obama also urged Democrats to be plain-spoken, and talked about aliens.
item.dc:creator: Tim Balk

item.title: Candidates for California Governor Debate Tonight: What to Know
item.description: Seven candidates in the California governor’s race will face off at a debate hosted by NBC Los Angeles. The New York Times will livestream the event.
item.dc:creator: Jill Cowan
item.media:description: Seven candidates engaged in a spirited debate hosted by CNN at East Los Angeles College on Tuesday.

item.title: A Democratic State Auditor Is Taking on Her Party, and Firing Up Voters
item.description: Massachusetts voters approved a ballot measure in 2024 authorizing Diana DiZoglio, the state auditor, to closely scrutinize the State Legislature. A battle ensued.
item.dc:creator: Jenna Russell
item.media:credit: Sophie Park for The New York Times
item.media:description: Diana DiZoglio, a Democrat, has faced stiff resistance from lawmakers in her own party.

item.title: U.S. Prosecutors Pull Out of Event in Sign of Rift With Legal Critics
item.description: Top federal prosecutors in New York withdrew from an annual event hosted by the City Bar Association, which has been critical of President Trump’s policies.
item.dc:creator: Jonah E. Bromwich and William K. Rashbaum

item.title: Justice Department Sues Colorado Over Gun Law
item.description: The lawsuit, which follows a separate suit the agency filed against the city of Denver on Tuesday, involves a state ban of high-capacity ammunition magazines.
item.dc:creator: Jack Healy
item.media:description: A custom-made semi-automatic hunting rifle with a high-capacity detachable magazine is displayed.

item.title: Suspect Michael Marx Charged With Shooting at Secret Service Agents Near National Mall
item.description: The suspect, a 45-year-old from Texas, faces three charges, including assaulting federal officers with a dangerous weapon.
item.dc:creator: Jacey Fortin
item.media:description: Police tape ringed the scene of the shooting in Washington on Monday.

item.title: Her Self-Experiment with Drug Detox Almost Broke Her
item.description: Against expert advice, people are using new and unpredictable synthetic drugs to experiment on themselves in hopes of becoming free of addiction.
item.dc:creator: Matt Richtel and Meridith Kohut
item.media:credit: Meridith Kohut for The New York Times

item.title: 3 People Are Evacuated From Cruise Ship With Hantavirus Outbreak
item.description: Three people with possible symptoms of the disease were evacuated on Wednesday morning from the Dutch-flagged MV Hondius.
item.dc:creator: Claire Moses and Aimee Ortiz
item.media:description: The MV Hondius off the port of Praia, the capital of Cape Verde, on Wednesday.

item.title: Should You Worry About Hantavirus?
item.description: Here’s what to know about the virus, how it spreads and the risk to the general public.
item.dc:creator: Nina Agrawal and Dani Blum

item.title: A Landslide in Alaska Set Off a Tsunami. There May Be More to Come.
item.description: Scientists say as glaciers retreat in a warming climate, landslide-generated tsunamis are likely to become more frequent.
item.dc:creator: Christian Elliott

item.title: New Study Shows Risks of Amazon Deforestation. And Rewards of Protection.
item.description: Researchers examined the combined effects of tree loss and global warming in an effort to better understand how and when an ecosystem collapse could unfold.
item.dc:creator: Sachi Kitajima Mulkey
item.media:description: A timber truck rolled through a deforested section of the Amazon in Pará State, Brazil, in November.

item.title: The Longevity Secrets Helping Athletes Blow Past the Limits of Age
item.description: With cutting-edge sports medicine and sci-fi gadgetry, more and more athletes are figuring out how to extend their careers.
item.dc:creator: Devin Gordon and Dina Litovsky

item.title: A Hotel in Venezuela’s Capital Has Become the U.S. Embassy’s De Facto Headquarters
item.description: The J.W. Marriott in Venezuela’s capital is suddenly awash in U.S. diplomats, spies and fortune seekers, showcasing changes in the country.
item.dc:creator: Simon Romero and Todd Heisler
item.media:description: The Marriott’s top floor serves as the de facto headquarters of the U.S. Embassy in Caracas.

item.title: Artifacts from the Titanic May Soon Be Auctioned
item.description: The last time a sale was proposed, the idea drew objections from the U.S. and French governments, and from UNESCO and other cultural institutions.
item.dc:creator: Marc Davis

item.title: In ‘Rocky Horror,’ Luke Evans Finds His Ballad of Sexual Liberation
item.description: What does it take to play Frank-N-Furter in “The Rocky Horror Show” on Broadway? Luke Evans transforms in five-inch heels and an endless supply of glitter.
item.dc:creator: Zachary Small and Justin J Wee
item.media:credit: Justin J Wee for The New York Times

item.title: Neil deGrasse Tyson: Give Us the Aliens
item.description: Is that too much to ask for?
item.dc:creator: Neil deGrasse Tyson
item.media:credit: Illustration by Sam Whitney/The New York Times

item.title: John Roberts Believes in an America That Doesn’t Exist
item.description: Guess who benefits from a “colorblind Constitution.”
item.dc:creator: Jamelle Bouie
item.media:credit: Tierney L. Cross/The New York Times

item.title: Tell Us How High Gas Prices Have Affected Your Finances
item.description: With fuel costs soaring from the war with Iran, we want to hear how you’re feeling about the U.S. economy.
item.dc:creator: Tony Romm
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:description: Gas prices have soared since the start of the war with Iran.

item.title: F.B.I. Director Kash Patel Criticizes How Nancy Guthrie Case Was Handled
item.description: Kash Patel went after local authorities on a podcast, saying the F.B.I. was kept out of the investigation for days. Sheriff Chris Nanos has denied that claim.
item.dc:creator: Reis Thebault
item.media:description: People look at the memorial set up near the driveway to Nancy Guthrie’s residence in Febraury in Tucson, Ariz.

item.title: His DNA Was Taken After His Arrest at an ICE Protest. Now, He’s Suing.
item.description: In a lawsuit, an Air Force veteran says that the federal government is sending a “chilling message” to people demonstrating against the immigration crackdown.
item.dc:creator: Hurubie Meko
item.media:credit: Jamie Kelter Davis for The New York Times
item.media:description: Demonstrators, who were being observed by federal law enforcement officials from a rooftop, gathered in September outside the Broadview ICE Detention Center in Chicago.

item.title: Office of Virginia State Senator Louise Lucas Searched by F.B.I. Agents
item.description: Two people familiar with the case said the search of a lawmaker’s business office was related to a Biden-era investigation of possible corruption and bribery related to marijuana dispensaries.
item.dc:creator: Devlin Barrett and Reid J. Epstein
item.media:description: State Senator L. Louise Lucas of Virginia, a Democrat, helped lead the push for a more aggressive congressional map meant to give her party 10 out of the state’s 11 seats in the U.S. House.

item.title: Clavicular Charged With Shooting at Alligator While Livestreaming
item.description: The influencer, whose real name is Braden Eric Peters, fired 12 shots at the gator from an airboat in the Florida Everglades.
item.dc:creator: David Ovalle and Christina Morales
item.category: Florida Fish and Wildlife Conservation Commission
item.media:credit: Cassidy Araiza for The New York Times
item.media:description: Clavicular streaming from the Odysea Aquarium in Scottsdale, Ariz., in February.
